title: Apparatuses, methods, and systems for starting an exciterless synchronous generator
abstract: Unique systems, methods, techniques and apparatuses of an exciterless synchronous generator are disclosed. One exemplary embodiment is an exciterless synchronous generator comprising a stator, a rotor, and a startup excitation system. The stator includes a set of stator windings. The rotor includes an energy harvest winding, a DC power supply including a DC bus and coupled to the energy harvest winding, and a field winding coupled to the DC power supply. The startup excitation system comprises one of a magnetic field generation system structured to generate a magnetic field received by the energy harvest winding in response to a rotation of the rotor, wherein the magnetic field is converted to DC power with the DC power supply and transmitted to the field winding; or a rotor DC power source including and diode coupled in series across the DC bus.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An exciterless synchronous generator comprising: a stator including a set of stator windings; a rotor including: an energy harvest winding, a DC power supply including a DC bus and coupled to the energy harvest winding, and a field winding coupled to the DC power supply; and a startup excitation system comprising one of: a magnetic field generation system structured to generate a magnetic field received by the energy harvest winding in response to a rotation of the rotor, wherein the magnetic field is converted to DC power with the DC power supply and transmitted to the field winding; or a rotor DC power source including and diode coupled in series across the DC bus. 2. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 1 wherein the magnetic field generation system includes a stator startup DC power supply coupled across the set of stator windings and including a switching device structured to controllably provide DC power from a stator DC power source to the set of stator windings while the rotor is rotating. 3. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 2 wherein the set of stator windings includes a first stator winding coupled in series with a second stator winding and a third stator winding is coupled in parallel with the second stator winding. 4. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 1 wherein the magnetic field generation system includes a plurality of permanent magnets operatively coupled to the rotor or to the stator. 5. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 4 wherein the rotor comprises a plurality of rotor poles and each of the plurality of permanent magnets are operatively coupled to a rotor pole of the plurality of rotor poles. 6. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 4 wherein the DC power supply includes a rectifier including a diode, and wherein each permanent magnet of the plurality of permanent magnets is sized such that the power received with the energy harvest winding includes a voltage magnitude greater than a forward bias voltage of the diode. 7. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 1 wherein the rotor comprises a plurality of energy harvest windings coupled to the DC power supply. 8. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 1 wherein the magnetic field generation system includes a stator startup AC power supply coupled across the set of stator windings and structured to controllably provide AC power to the set of stator windings while the rotor is rotating. 9. An exciterless synchronous generator comprising: a stator including a set of stator windings; a rotor including: an energy harvest winding, a DC power supply including a DC bus and coupled to the energy harvest winding, and a field winding coupled to the DC power supply; and a startup excitation system comprising one of: a passive startup system incorporated into the rotor structured to provide DC power to the field winding during a startup operation; or a stator startup DC power supply coupled across the set of stator windings and including a switching device structured to controllably provide DC power from a stator DC power source to the set of stator windings while the rotor is rotating during startup operation effective to generate a magnetic field received by the energy harvest winding in response to the rotation of the rotor. 10. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 9 wherein the passive startup system includes a rotor DC power source and a diode coupled in series across the DC bus and structured to provide DC power to the field winding in response to a DC bus voltage generated by power received from the energy harvest winding decreasing below a voltage across the rotor DC power source. 11. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 10 wherein the rotor DC power source comprises at least one of a battery, a battery bank, a rechargeable battery, a capacitor, and a supercapacitor. 12. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 9 wherein the set of stator windings includes a first stator winding coupled in series with a second stator winding and a third stator winding is coupled in parallel with the second stator winding. 13. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 9 wherein the passive startup system comprises a plurality of permanent magnets operatively coupled to an outer surface of the rotor. 14. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 13 wherein the rotor comprises a plurality of rotor poles and each of the plurality of permanent magnets is operatively coupled to one rotor pole of the plurality of rotor poles. 15. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 13 wherein the DC power supply includes a rectifier including a diode, and wherein each of the plurality of permanent magnets is sized such that the power received with the energy harvest winding includes a voltage magnitude greater than a forward bias voltage of the diode. 16. The exciterless synchronous generator of claim 9 wherein the stator startup DC power supply includes a diode or a current limiting device coupled in series with the stator DC power source. 17. A method comprising: 

 operating an exciterless synchronous generator comprising: a stator including a set of stator windings, a rotor including: an energy harvest winding, a DC power supply including a DC bus and coupled to the energy harvest winding, and a field winding coupled to the DC power supply, a startup excitation system comprising one of: a magnetic field generation system structured to generate a magnetic field received by the energy harvest winding in response to a rotation of the rotor, wherein the magnetic field is converted to DC power with the DC power supply and transmitted to the field winding, or a rotor DC power source including and diode coupled in series across the DC bus; increasing a voltage across the DC bus during a startup operation mode using the startup excitation system; and operating the exciterless synchronous generator in a normal operation mode in response to the voltage across the DC bus exceeding a threshold voltage sufficient to operate the exciterless synchronous generator at a synchronous speed. 18. The method of claim 17 wherein the magnetic field generation system includes a stator startup DC power supply coupled across the set of stator windings and a switching device structured to controllably provide DC power from a stator DC power source to the set of stator windings while the rotor is rotating. 19. The method of claim 18 wherein the set of stator windings includes a first stator winding coupled in series with a parallel coupled second and third stator winding. 20. The method of claim 17 wherein the magnetic field generation system includes a plurality of permanent magnets operatively coupled to the rotor or to the stator. 21. The method of claim 20 wherein the rotor comprises a plurality of rotor poles and each permanent magnet of the plurality of permanent magnets are operatively coupled to one rotor pole of the plurality of rotor poles, wherein the DC power supply includes a rectifier including a diode, and wherein each permanent magnet of the plurality of permanent magnets are sized such that the power received with the energy harvest winding includes a voltage greater than a forward bias voltage of the diode. 22. The method of claim 17 wherein the magnetic field generation system includes a stator startup AC power supply coupled across the set of stator windings and structured to controllably provide AC power to the set of stator windings while the rotor is rotating.
